column1: Contribution Evaluation
column2:
PREPARATION PHASE
To understand the work that different people in ALANA go through we require template workflows. The following examples attempt to unravel the mysteries.
column5:
ESTIMATION PHASE
Before initiating any work, we evaluate the scope, requirements, and expected outcomes. This evaluation includes scoring the project based on its reach, impact, and the effort required.
column9:
EXECUTION PHASE
This phase involves carrying out the tasks necessary for project completion. It requires effective coordination among team members to meet the set objectives.
column12:
REVIEW PHASE
Upon completing the work, we measure audience impact and distribute a bonus upon how the project impacts the real world/ audience reception.

column1: Contribution Category
column2:
ALANA REFERENCING BRANCH
Select the ALANA Branch your task belongs to
column3: Small Workflow Description
column4: Tools
column5:
Reach
Number of people affected (summarizing current follower amount on social media divided by the different accounts provides a rough estimate)

For Procuts the actual number of consumers will be another additional indicator if >= 100 people
column6:
Impact on an organizational Level
3 = massive
2 = high impact
1 = medium impact
0.5 = low impact
0.25 = minimal impact
column7:
Impact on a customer Level
3 = massive
2 = high impact
1 = medium impact
0.5 = low impact
0.25 = minimal impact
column8:
Battery Life Bar
Imagine a battery and decide how much of that battery gets drained to perform the task at hand.

XS  = 1 - 10P in %
S   = 11 - 30P in %
M   = 31 - 50P in %
L   = 51 - 70P in %
XL  = 71 - 90P in %
XXL = 91 - 100P in %

The user needs to input this personally and the Nucleus Guardian would have the ability to adjust this one value only.
column9:
Alignment and Quality Control (estimate)
Alignment = On-brand delivery which means delivering a concept that is recognizable and propels the ALANA brand vision as well as its according visuals!

Quality = in comparison to similar products/ spectrum of comparable products out there

poor = 0 - 29% 
fair quality = 30 - 49%
good = 50 - 64%
very good = 65 - 84%
excellent = 85 - 100%

The user needs to input this personally and the Nucleus Guardian would have the ability to adjust this one value only.
column10:
Interim Result
Final XP Result takes the total ranking/ influence under perspective:

Infrastructure & Strategy = Rank 7
Onboarding = Rank 6
Brand Identity & Socials = Rank 5
----------------------
ALANAmagazine = Rank 4
ALANA's Boutique = Rank 4
ALANA's World = Rank 4
ALANA AI = Rank 4
column11:
AVERAGE MULTIPLIER
Add if a task is done more than once to fit the calculation. If a task is repeated, not every task will require the same amount of effort in a series. Hence, we call it "average multiplier." These are the available multipliers:

1-5   Repetitions = *3
6-10  Repetitions = *5
11-20 Repetitions = *8
> 20  Repetitions = *13
column12:
Tipping Bonus
Rewarding work that has been better than expected. The default option is not getting a bonus. 

0%  = No Tip
5%  = Small Tip
15% = Medium Tip
20% = Big Tip
25% = Huge Tip
column13:
Final Result Evaluation
Interim Result * Tipping Bonus (if applicable)

column1:

* Example calculation based on the Nucleus Guardian role description

column3:
What does it take to write and submit a DAO Proposal?
- Investigation in the dedicated proposal topic (can be technical, require work with third parties, purely investigative, etc...)
- Open up a new conversation thread in the DAC Discussions channels. 
- Create a new template and fill in all the data as seen in former examples.
- Involve other people from your dedicated branch and wider community to create a proposal that will meet the quorum by utilizing the formerly mentioned communication channel.
- If necessary build working groups.
- Finally, post the finished proposal on Discord/ Snapshot and bring attention to it by communicating the active proposal.
- Implementing a DAO Proposal is another task and can look very unique depending on what is being proposed!

column1:
Management of Legal Protection for ALANA

* Example calculation based on Terms & Conditions around ALANAmagazine submissions
column3:
What does it take to write drafts around legal protection for ALANA and the different branches and coordinate those with our lawyers?
- Review existing community rights laws and frameworks.
- Identify and conduct more profound research on the legal issues at hand that need solving or protection.
- Inform yourself about relevant local, state, and federal regulations, considering where ALANA is incorporated.
- Outline the main sections of legal protection needed by writing an initial draft for the issue at hand.
- Reach out to an ALANA lawyer and introduce the issue as well as sending your draft.
- Schedule a meeting with an ALANA lawyer to discuss potential adjustments and finalizations of the draft.
- Refine the final draft with the lawyer, ensure alignment with existing laws and regulations, and strengthen language to maximize legal protection.
- Present draft legal documents to community members for input and do another feedback loop if required.
- Implement the new legal draft on the platforms or public forums where needed.
column4:
What tools are required?
- Access to Google Sheets
- Access to Zoom or similar
- Proficient language knowledge in Spanish (ALANA is incorporated in Peru and bound to the local laws)

column1: Organizational/ Structural Design
column3:
What does it take to create/ maintain/ innovate on ALANA's organizational/ structural design?
- A general understanding of the existing structure
- A thorough analyzation of the current organization and its failing/ weak elements
- An inquiry with the DAO members about satisfaction/ dissatisfaction - a set of interviews or a questionnaire that identifies the needs for the community
- Definition of new roles or branches according the evaluated data points (polls, questionnaires, interviews, etc.)
- A clear definition of roles and/ or additional branches
- A clear argument for or against certain roles/ branches
- Establish clear expectations if new roles or branches are added
- Designing a flow in a visual format that bridges the gap from text to visual mind and clearly shows the benefits
- Create a DAO Proposal for the new strucutre in a text format supported by visual elements
- If the DAO proposal is accepted establish the changes to the ALANA organization onchain with HatsProtocol x Unlock Protocol
- Officially announce the changes and prepare according copy for it
- Help guide people towards the established changes (can be personal calls, public sessions like livestreams or answering questions in the chats)
column4:
What tools are required?
- Access to Discord
- Access to Figma
- Access to Google Sheets
- Access to HatsProtocol

column1: Writing Interviews/ Feedback Initiatives
column3:
What does it take to write interviews and run feedback initiatives?
* Recommended reference: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=f_tGpfXX2ww

- Research the interviewee and topic thoroughly
- Develop a list of key questions (open-ended, probing, follow-up)
- Coordinate an interview time
- Develop an incentive idea for the interviewee
- Create a logical structure for the interview consisting of an introduction (brief and purpose) and a question structure (start with broader questions to more specific ones)
- Practice active listening - allow for pauses and silences to give the interviewee time to think as well as maintain an organic flow
- Ensure the interviewee is really heard and consider final thoughts
- Review and organize your notes
- Identify key quotes and themes
- Evaluate and compare responses
- Structure the written piece in a compelling narrative format
- Suggest active resolutions from the provided feedback results
column4:
What tools are required?
- Access to Word or similar
- Access to a meeting coordination tool such as Calendly or Lettucemeet
- Access to a form designer tool like deform

column1:
Educational Article Writing + Social Media Content Writing

* Example calculation based on mirror.xyz articles
column3:
What does it take to write an educational article?
We recommend following the 7 steps of "The Art of Explanation" by Ros Atkins as it leads to convincing results.
- Set-up:Ask the right questions about your audience and what exactly you want them to understand.
- Find the information: Gather initial information to understand the topic better and identify key points to cover
- Distil the information: Break it down to the most necessary parts of information needed and remind yourself of the original purpose of this article
- Organize the information: All information comes in sections, make sure the sections are ordered in a meaninful way to convey the core of your story 
- Link the information: Link the information for credibility and literally but also ensure it works in alignment with The ALANA Project brand and brand voice
- Tighten: Take a step away from your article and make sure you are using clear and simple examples to explain. This is like a last step over analyzation to make sure the content is delivered in the best way possible.
- Delivery: Create befitting visuals and deliver in multiple formats across ALANA's socials.
column4:
What tools are required?
- Google Sheets or similar
- Google Docs or similar
- AI Generator for images
- mirror.xyz access
- Social media accounts for outreach

column1:

column3:
What does it take to re-design or maintain existing community platforms?
- Understand the pain points of the current experience and ask for help from the Brand Identity & Socials team to understand the target audience even better.
- Gather insights from the current audience via a survey and evaluate feedback.
- With the new data in hand, plan the layout and user flow of the new experience or adjustments in Milanote, Figma, or similar.
- Adjustments or maintenance can also include security updates or the integration of new automation/ bots and might require additional skills and understanding.
- Open a public conversation about the new experience and gather feedback.
- Refine the experience with the user feedback gathered and implemented. 
- Implement the adjustments as planned.
- Keep refining as needed and conduct frequent audits to ensure seamless user flow.

column1:
Product Website Design

column2: Brand Identity & Socials, ALANAmagazine, ALANA AI, ALANAboutique, ALANAworld
column3:
What does it take to design & mock-up a product website for ALANA?
- Define website goals and target audience and factor in the overall ALANA brand identity.
- Outline content requirements for each page and sub-pages.
- Sketch rough layouts for the homepage and sub-pages and factor in the user flow and navigational structure.
- Use the existing brand identity for color schemes and typographic elements. Create consistent UI elements (buttons, forms, icons).
- Design hover states and micro-interactions. Plan for any interactive features (e.g., product galleries, FAQ accordions).
- Design high-fidelity prototypes (mock-ups) for the homepage and sub-pages in Figma.
- Don't forget to create mockups for responsive layouts (desktop, tablet, mobile)
- Write compelling copy for each page and source or create product images and graphics. Develop any necessary diagrams or infographics.
- Gather feedback from community members and refine the mock-up according to the feedback received.
- Prepare final mock-ups for handoff to development team.
column4:

- GitHub Team Access
- Access to Figma
- Access to other required tools or resources

column1:
ALANAmagazine Editorial Curation and Management

* Example calculation based on an AI tutorial curation
column3:
What does it take to curate and manage a Fashion Editorial for ALANAmagazine?
(Every ALANAmagazine has four fashion editorials. The Battery Life Bar value is essential to ensure fair output values. We recommend using 50%+ for AI Editorials and 80%+ for 3D Editorials.)
- Develop the editorial concept and theme with the current edition's theme in mind.
- Create a mood board to visualize the editorial direction. 
- Submit your credentials as art directors to the ALANAmagazine team, including bio, links, and a high-res portrait image of yourself (preferably B/W).
- For 3D Editorials: Create a 3D environment that will serve as the rendering environment for the fashion editorial.
- For 3D Editorials: Create the model or models you aim to use for the final renderings.
- A timeline needs to be established depending on the submission type (AI or 3D files).
- Provide all necessary information to the ALANAmagazine website team so a submission page can be established containing all necessary indications for the community to submit their work. 
- Upon the submission deadline, ensure a fair selection process for the winning submissions.
- For AI Editorials: Artdirect the final curation list and provide indications for lighting, color grading, and any other type of formatting to the ALANAmagazine team.

column1: ALANAmagazine Interview Coordination

- Ensure you have the necessary recording devices, note-taking tools, and any other equipment needed for the interview
- Capture the interview either through recording (with permission) or by taking detailed notes to reference later
- After the interview, reach out if you need further clarification on any points discussed

column1:
ALANAmagazine Article Writing

* is already incentivized with $0.15/ word
column3:
What does it take to write an article for ALANAmagazine?
- If the article is based on a previously recorded interview, convert the conversation into text for easier reference and to facilitate the writing process
------
- If the article is not a recorded interview, select a relevant topic from the content curation sheet
- Gather initial information to understand the topic better and identify key points to cover
- Compile a list of credible sources, including books, articles, interviews, and expert opinions
- If applicable, include images, infographics, or other visuals to enhance the content and break up text
- Write the first draft based on your outline, including an engaging introduction, body paragraphs, a conclusion and potentially direct quotes
- Review the draft for clarity, coherence, and flow, making necessary revisions to improve the overall quality
- Check for grammatical errors, typos, and formatting issues to ensure the article is polished and professional
- Share the article with an editor or peer for constructive feedback before final submission
- Make any final adjustments based on feedback and submit the article according to the magazine's guidelines

What tools are required?

- Google Docs or similar
- Social media accounts for outreach

- Organize the main points and structure the short-form content logically, keeping it brief and to the point
- If applicable, include images, infographics, or other visuals to enhance the content and break up text
- Revise the draft to eliminate unnecessary words and ensure the message is clear and impactful
- Check for grammatical errors, typos, and formatting issues to ensure professionalism
- Send the short-form content to an editor or peer for feedback before finalizing it for publication
column4:

column1: ALANAmagazine Fact-Checking & Content Editing
column3:
What does it take to fact-check and edit content for ALANAmagazine?
- Ensure the article arrives with sources provided, if not, check back with the original author
- Confirm the sources are correct and reliable/ accurate
- Read the article and check for any claims made; if anything is unclear confirm with the original author
- Provide feedback to the original author and ask for changes if applicable 
- Read the article thoroughly to understand its content, structure, and intent
- Ensure the writing is clear and consistent in tone, style, and terminology
- Assess the organization of the article, ensuring it has a logical flow with a strong introduction, body, and conclusion
- Remove any redundant or irrelevant information that does not contribute to the main message
- Ensure visuals fit the aesthetic and quality standard of the magazine and enhance the article they are paired with
- Check for grammatical errors, typos, and punctuation mistakes using tools and manual proofreading
- Offer constructive feedback to the writer, highlighting strengths and areas for improvement
- Implement all necessary changes and prepare the article for publication, ensuring it meets the magazine’s standards
- Hand the article over to the layouter of the magazine
column4:
What tools are required?
- PDF Reader and annotation tool
- Google Docs or similar

column1: ALANAmagazine Layout Design
column3:
What does it take to layout ALANAmagazine?
- Ensure the overall design is in complete alignment with The ALANA Project branding
- Ensure the layouter has advanced knowledge on utilizing Adobe InDesign for layouting the magazine
- Develop a style guide that includes fonts, colors, and design elements to ensure consistency throughout the magazine
- Design a grid system to organize content effectively, balancing text and images for optimal readability
- Use headings, subheadings, and varying font sizes to guide readers through the content and highlight important information
- Ensure body text is legible by choosing appropriate font sizes, line spacing, and paragraph breaks
- Strategically use white space to enhance the layout, making it visually appealing and easier to navigate
- Select high-quality images and graphics that complement the text and enhance the overall aesthetic (if the quality standard is off, utilize AI enhancer tools to adapt)
- Adjust layouts for print and digital versions, considering factors like interactivity for digital formats and bleed for print.
- Ensure all files are set to the correct dimensions, resolution (300 dpi), and color mode (CMYK) for printing.
- Hand over a draft file for proofreading, ensuring all elements are aligned and visually cohesive.
- Export the final layout in appropriate formats (PDF for print, interactive formats for digital) and distribute as needed.
column4:

- InDesign
- PDF Reader
- Photoshop
- AI Enhancer

column3:
What does it take to reach out to reach out to potential sponsors and partners for ALANAmagazine?
- Research and select companies or brands that align with your magazine’s values and audience
- Utilize the Media Kit to demonstrate the value of your magazine to potential sponsors
- Write a clear and concise email or letter that outlines the sponsorship opportunity, including what you are asking for and what you can offer in return
- Emphasize how the partnership can benefit the sponsor, such as increased brand exposure and access to your audience.
- If you don’t receive a response, send a polite follow-up email to remind them of your proposal and express your interest in discussing it further
- Be prepared to discuss different sponsorship levels and tailor your offerings to meet the sponsor's needs
- Keep in touch with potential sponsors, providing updates on your magazine and any relevant opportunities for collaboration
- After securing sponsorships, track the performance of the partnership and report back to sponsors on the outcomes, reinforcing the value of their investment

What does it take to curate an individual exhibition space for ALANAboutique?
(ALANAboutique consists of individual exhibition spaces. Some are unique and require more dedicated development such as educational spaces. We recommend setting a battery life bar value of 55%. For shopping spaces we recommend setting a lower value for about 35%.)
Brainstorm ideas for relevant educational topics for the ALANA community. If needed, seek help from the Brand Identity & Socials team regarding a defined target audience.
- Conduct in-depth research on the selected educational topic and bring in people from the community who might have more knowledge on the specific topic.
- Select representative artworks and additional materials to showcase. Ask the Infrastructure & Strategy team for a legal document to obtain the original authors' permission to do so.

- Forward the high-quality exhibition pieces and their descriptions to the ALANAboutique virtual space design team.

- Plan interactive elements to engage visitors (e.g., zoom features, 360-degree views and similar). Provide the information as well to the ALANAboutique design team.
- For the final publication, please write a short press release about that specific place containing a summarized version and ensure all contributors are named and linked in the document.

- Research the platform to which the space gets implemented so the framework is clear and well understood.
- Design and model the larger 3D environment by factoring in the existing branded design elements. 
- Develop unique textures that fit ALANA's brand identity and apply them. 
- Create 3D models and assets to outfit the space partially and section off individual exhibition spaces.
- Optimize the space and individual models for web deployment and implement level-of-detail (LOD) as well as texture compression.
- Create final renderings of the seasonal exhibition and from each (empty) individual space for marketing purposes!

What tools are required?
- Access to the AI Model/ GitHub
- Access to community platforms/ API keys

column1: Fixing bugs in AI Assistant
column3:
What does it take to fix bugs in the AI Assistant?
- fix defects in any reported issues in Alana AI
- fix regressions (previously working systems exhibiting issues due to changes)
- apply security patches and fixes as they occur
- write tests to confirm and prove bugs have been fixed. add to unit tests for continous monitoring to prevent regressions
column4:
What tools are required?
- Access to the AI Model/ GitHub
- Access to an easy to use data base

column1: Performance improvements to AI Assistant
column3:
What does it take to improve performance in an AI Assistant?
- Improve user experience by reducing latency between initiating an action and seeing a response.
- Faster streaming of LLM responses
- Faster search and database retrieval
- Algorithmic changes/refactors that improve system performance and robustness
- Any software change that improves performance according to metrics that can be observed.
- Demonstrate improvements using objective metrics - eg., time to complete task, latency, success rates for task completion

column1: Market and recruting for contributions to AI Assistant
column3:
What does it take to market and recruit contributions to the AI Assistant?
- find creatives that would be willing to contribute materials and knowledge to the Alana AI
- explain and educate potential contributors of benefits, protections and compensation for their contributions
- develop and execute on recruitment strategies for targetted contributors including outreach in community forums, meetups
- define metric for measuring success of initiatives - eg., new user signups, sales made, etc.

column1: Adding and maintaining content and knowledge accessible to the AI Assistant
column3:
What does it take to add/maintain content in an AI Assistant?
- Onboard and review candidate content from contributors for errors, copyright violations, and other policy violations
- Screen and check for security exploits and malicious code/content
- Work with contributors to provide feedback if content not acceptable
- Modify/edit content to ensure works within our Alana AI system
- Automate tests so they can be constantly re-run with every change in the system

column1: Maintain good quality assurance in Alana AI
column3:
What does it take to maintain good quality assurance in Alana AI?
- Periodically test system on a good representative range of tasks to see if system response if of sufficent good quality
- Detect regressions in behavior where system behavior has worsened and file bug issues to fix
- Design, write and create test plans or evals to help the automate and measure the systems key quality performance indices
- Monitor user transactions to detect anomalous or good working patterns to suggest new features or fixes

column1:
Adding Novel Features to the FABA AI Agentic system 

* Example calculation based on the Comfy UI integration
column3:
What does it take to add novel features to the AI Assistant?
- Draft and write a PRD (Product Requirements Document) for a high level proposal describing a new feature, justifying the need for it with supporting arguments, including current pain points and how it would enhance the user experience.
- Design features include front-end user experience and back-end components
- Clarify acceptance criteria for successfully implemented feature
- Create plan for implementation, including task breakdown and time/resource estimates

column1: Add Novel Character Development to an AI Assistant
column3:
What does it take to add novel character development to AI Assistant
- Ensure ALANA AI always follows the Alana Manifesto guidelines
- Design curriculum for training Alana with new emerging content
- Ensure Alana AI's personality does not have malicious or undesirable properties inconsistent with the Manifesto
- Maintain Alana's core personality and ensure it does not evolve or exhibit undesirable properties
